# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a simple Dockerfile for our Go microservice. Compile the application and run the binary. Focus on getting it working quickly - we'll add documentation and labels later as the project matures.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Golang runtime image to avoid dependency conflicts across different base images in Dockerfiles, e.g., Ubuntu Base Image or Alpine Linux Based Images 
FROM golang:1.16-alpine3

WORKDIR /app

# Copy the Go application binary into our docker container at runtime (this step is done to handle dependencies)
COPY go/ ./go/
RUN CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux go build -a -installsuffix cgo -o main ./.  # Compile and create a standalone executable file. We do not use "ldflags" as it can cause issues in case the app has dependencies on system-wide installed libraries
# Expose port to access application from outside of container for debugging purposes or other needs, e.g., `PORT=80`  (also this will be exposed at runtime) but you could expose only if needed with a different strategy like env vars in Dockerfile below the above COPY statement
EXPOSE $PORT # Expose port to access application from outside of container for debugging purposes or other needs, e.g., `PORT=80`  (also this will be exposed at runtime) but you could expose only if needed with a different strategy like env vars in Dockerfile below the above COPY statement
# Label our image and use it as an argument for entrypoint to run main function when container is started. This way we have binary inside of docker instance, so there's no need on local system or other containers  you can build your own images from this one then start services with them without running entire application code
LABEL maintainers="your-name <email@example.com>" # Provide author and email for the image (optional) to help others find yours, see https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/commandline/tag/#tag-a-repository---including-the-tagged-image
LABEL description='A small dockerized go application.' # Provide a brief summary of your image (optional) to help others understand what the purpose is, see https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/commandline/tag/#describe--including-all-tags  for more details
LABEL version='1.0'   	# Define an application specific or project related label if needed (optional) to help others understand what the image is intended towards, see https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/commandline/tag/#describe--including-all-tags  for more details
ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/sh", "-c"] # The command that will be run when docker container starts (this step to prevent the main process from hanging indefinitely)   Run as a sh -c and pass everything after this argument into it. Here we call shell, which runs /app inside our Dockerfile with all necessary flags
# Copy rest of your application's content here if there is any – e.g., source code or static files for serving via nginx in production mode below the above EXPOSE statement and before COPY command (if you want to copy them into container)   You can use this pattern as a starting point
# Rest of your Dockerfile goes here, add any commands required – e.g., installing dependencies using apt-get or yum depending on os in the FROM line above for simplicity and clarity about which packages will be installed by default (if needed). Also remember to expose port if you want other services/containers inside this container
